summary: The Docker build failed because a `wget` command inside the Dockerfile could not download the nydus binary from GitHub, receiving an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error. This caused the `buildx bake` command to fail with exit code 1.
root_cause: A transient network error (HTTP 502 Bad Gateway) from GitHub prevented the wget download of the nydus static binary at https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz during the Docker build step.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: This is the direct error from wget showing a transient network failure when connecting to GitHub to download the nydus binary.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This confirms the wget process failed with exit code 1 due to the 502 error, causing the Docker build step to fail.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the final GitHub Actions error annotation confirming the buildx bake command failed due to the wget network error.
This identifies the exact Dockerfile line (line 336) that triggered the failure.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient network failure (HTTP 502 from GitHub). If the issue persists, consider adding retry logic to the wget command in Dockerfile line 336 (e.g., `wget --retry-connrefused --tries=3`) or mirroring the nydus release artifact to a more reliable host.

summary: The functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because GitHub's servers returned repeated 502 errors when pip tried to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time due to the same transient network issue.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated HTTP 502 responses when the test attempted to download a package from github.com, causing pip to exhaust its retries and exit with code 1. This is a transient network failure, not a code defect.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that GitHub returned too many 502 responses, which is a transient server-side network failure.
The test was retried 3 times (via pytest-rerunfailures) and failed all 3 times, confirming a persistent transient network issue rather than a fluke.
The final test failure is an assertion that pip returned a non-zero exit code, which was caused by the network error above.
Confirms the test was making a real network request to GitHub, making it susceptible to transient network failures.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job; the failure is caused by transient 502 errors from GitHub and is not related to any code change. Consider adding a longer retry delay or marking this test to be skipped when GitHub is unreachable.

summary: A Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts` because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected the URL without a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This caused 1 test suite and 1 test to fail, resulting in exit code 1.
root_cause: The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` in `test/config/get.ts` failed because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with trailing slash) but the test expected `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (without trailing slash).
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the failing test suite and test name.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: the received value has a trailing slash that the expected value does not.
Shows the test assertion at line 32 of test/config/get.ts that failed.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test suite and 1 test failed overall.
Confirms the jest run exited with status 1, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Fix the `pnpm config get` command (or the config reading logic) to strip the trailing slash from registry URLs when outputting them, so the output matches the expected value `https://my-org.registry.example.com` without a trailing slash.
